unit01.title: Module 1 - Design History & Foundations
unit01.summary: From hand-lettered manuscripts to movable type and modern design. Students learn how visual communication evolved and how form serves function.
unit01.description: A fast-paced overview of design’s evolution — illuminated manuscripts, printing, Bauhaus, and the digital era — emphasizing how designers solve communication problems through structure, hierarchy, and clarity.

chapters.description: Before Print — hierarchy, illuminated letters, and how designers communicated meaning without type.

lessons.title: What Is Graphic Design?
lessons.overview: Students explore what graphic design really means, identify examples in daily life, and discuss how designers communicate through visuals.
lessons.objective1: Define graphic design as visual communication.
lessons.objective2: Identify examples of design in everyday life.
lessons.objective3: Discuss how visuals can convey tone and message.

lessons.title: Design Detective
lessons.overview: Students analyze real designs to uncover how color, type, and layout communicate meaning. They learn to see like a designer.
lessons.objective1: Identify visual elements that communicate emotion or tone.
lessons.objective2: Describe how composition influences meaning.
lessons.objective3: Use vocabulary of design principles in discussion.

lessons.title: Mood Poster Challenge
lessons.overview: Students create a poster that conveys a mood or theme using imagery, color, and composition—no words allowed.
lessons.objective1: Use visual hierarchy and balance to guide attention.
lessons.objective2: Apply color and imagery to express emotion.
lessons.objective3: Collaborate and critique effectively.

lessons.title: Elements of Design: CARP Principles
lessons.overview: Students learn Contrast, Alignment, Repetition, and Proximity (CARP) and apply these principles in layout and design analysis.
lessons.objective1: Explain each CARP principle with examples.
lessons.objective2: Apply design principles to improve composition.
lessons.objective3: Evaluate peer designs for clarity and balance.

chapters.description: Foundations of Visual Design — building the designer’s toolkit.

lessons.title: Color Theory
lessons.overview: Students explore color relationships and psychology, learning how hue, saturation, and value shape mood and readability.
lessons.objective1: Identify primary, secondary, and complementary colors.
lessons.objective2: Create a color palette that communicates mood.
lessons.objective3: Apply color harmony to a simple design.

lessons.title: Typography Basics: Serif vs Sans Serif
lessons.overview: Students explore type classifications, readability, and how font choice changes the tone of a design.
lessons.objective1: Differentiate serif and sans-serif typefaces.
lessons.objective2: Select fonts appropriate for a given purpose.
lessons.objective3: Analyze how typography affects brand perception.

lessons.title: Typography Basics: House Identity
lessons.overview: Students create a consistent personal type style (house style) to represent their creative identity.
lessons.objective1: Select fonts that express personal or brand identity.
lessons.objective2: Maintain consistent font use across multiple designs.
lessons.objective3: Explain stylistic choices using design vocabulary.

lessons.title: Typography Basics: Hierarchy
lessons.overview: Students learn how font size, weight, and spacing create structure and guide the viewer’s eye.
lessons.objective1: Use typographic scale to establish hierarchy.
lessons.objective2: Apply spacing and alignment for visual flow.
lessons.objective3: Design a layout emphasizing readable hierarchy.

unit02.title: Module 2 - Branding, Heraldry, and Identity
unit02.summary: Students explore how symbols, color, and structure communicate identity—from medieval crests to modern brands.
unit02.description: This module connects history, symbolism, and branding. Students study heraldry, analyze modern logo systems, and create identity designs through research, iteration, and reflection.

chapters.description: The Language of Logos — from symbols of heritage to symbols of commerce.

lessons.title: Logos & Branding
lessons.overview: Students learn the Four Qualities of Effective Logo Design—simplicity, memorability, versatility, and appropriateness—and explore how logos communicate personality and values.
lessons.objective1: Identify characteristics of strong logo design.
lessons.objective2: Analyze brand personality through visual cues.
lessons.objective3: Compare logos using the four-qualities framework.

lessons.title: The Art of Logo Design
lessons.overview: Students study the five types of logos (Wordmark, Lettermark, Symbol/Icon, Combination Mark, Emblem) and sketch their own brand concepts.
lessons.objective1: Differentiate between logo types and purposes.
lessons.objective2: Generate design concepts inspired by the five types.
lessons.objective3: Apply typography and color choices to reinforce brand identity.

lessons.title: Logo Design Challenge
lessons.overview: Students design a logo for a fictional coffee shop, creating five logo types with two iterations each to show feedback and refinement.
lessons.objective1: Create five logo variations for a single brand.
lessons.objective2: Demonstrate iteration and peer feedback.
lessons.objective3: Evaluate designs using the four logo qualities.

chapters.description: Heraldry & Symbolism — the history of graphic design through identity and storytelling.

lessons.title: The Origins of Design: Heraldry and the Birth of Logos
lessons.overview: Students trace the evolution of identity design from medieval family crests to modern brands, exploring how early symbols represented values and allegiance.
lessons.objective1: Explain how heraldry influenced modern branding.
lessons.objective2: Identify parallels between coats of arms and logo design.
lessons.objective3: Analyze symbols as forms of visual storytelling.

lessons.title: Anatomy of a Coat of Arms
lessons.overview: Students learn the traditional parts of a coat of arms and how arrangement and symbolism communicate meaning.
lessons.objective1: Identify the core components of a heraldic crest.
lessons.objective2: Interpret how arrangement and shape convey hierarchy.
lessons.objective3: Label and describe real-world crests and their symbolism.

lessons.title: Symbolism & Color in Heraldry
lessons.overview: Students study the meanings behind heraldic colors and symbols, learning how visual language encodes values and traits.
lessons.objective1: Match heraldic colors and symbols with their meanings.
lessons.objective2: Select colors and icons to represent personal values.
lessons.objective3: Apply symbolic thinking to design decisions.

lessons.title: Create Your Own Crest: Sketch & Symbol Plan
lessons.overview: Students brainstorm, sketch, and plan a personal or family crest that represents their values, interests, and identity.
lessons.objective1: Plan a crest layout with meaningful symbols and color.
lessons.objective2: Justify design choices in writing and discussion.
lessons.objective3: Refine sketches based on peer feedback.

lessons.title: Create Your Own Crest: Digital Design
lessons.overview: Students recreate their crest digitally using Canva or Adobe Express, applying CARP principles, color theory, and typography.
lessons.objective1: Digitally render a complete heraldic crest design.
lessons.objective2: Apply alignment, contrast, and spacing for balance.
lessons.objective3: Maintain symbolic consistency in final design.

lessons.title: Crest Gallery & Reflection
lessons.overview: Students present their completed crests in a gallery walk and reflect on how their design represents identity and visual storytelling.
lessons.objective1: Present and explain personal design decisions.
lessons.objective2: Reflect on how symbolism communicates identity.
lessons.objective3: Provide constructive feedback during peer review.

chapters.description: Brand Implementation — extend identity across print and digital touchpoints.

lessons.title: Brand Board & Visual Identity Kit
lessons.overview: Students build a Brand Board that unifies logo, color palette, typography, and imagery to ensure consistency across designs.
lessons.objective1: Assemble a cohesive brand board.
lessons.objective2: Document color, type, and usage rules.
lessons.objective3: Demonstrate consistency across mockups.

lessons.title: Advertisement Design Basics
lessons.overview: Students learn composition and layout principles used in posters, flyers, and social media ads (balance, white space, focal point).
lessons.objective1: Design layouts emphasizing focal point and flow.
lessons.objective2: Use whitespace and alignment effectively.
lessons.objective3: Prepare assets for different sizes/ratios.

lessons.title: Social Media Branding Challenge
lessons.overview: Students adapt their brand for social media by designing matching post, story, and banner graphics.
lessons.objective1: Maintain brand consistency across formats.
lessons.objective2: Optimize designs for multiple aspect ratios.
lessons.objective3: Export correctly for digital distribution.

lessons.title: Print vs Digital Design
lessons.overview: Students compare design considerations for print (CMYK, bleed, resolution) vs digital (RGB, screen ratio) and redesign an ad for both.
lessons.objective1: Explain key differences between print and digital.
lessons.objective2: Prepare files with correct color spaces and bleed.
lessons.objective3: Adapt one design to both mediums.

lessons.title: Mini Brand Campaign Project
lessons.overview: Students create a 3-piece ad campaign (poster, banner, social post) for their brand or a real school club.
lessons.objective1: Apply color, type, hierarchy, and branding cohesively.
lessons.objective2: Demonstrate consistency across multiple deliverables.
lessons.objective3: Reflect on audience and message effectiveness.

unit03.title: Module 3 - Visual Storytelling & Multimedia Production
unit03.summary: Students integrate narrative, motion, and sound to produce short multimedia pieces.
unit03.description: This module introduces story structure, storyboarding, motion graphics fundamentals, and multimedia production culminating in a short promotional video.

chapters.description: From static to motion — plan, animate, and present.

lessons.title: Introduction to Visual Storytelling
lessons.overview: Students explore how images, text, and sound combine to tell a story and draft a storyboard for a short promo.
lessons.objective1: Identify components of a simple visual narrative.
lessons.objective2: Plan a sequence using a storyboard.
lessons.objective3: Align message and audience in concepting.

lessons.title: Storyboard & Layout Design
lessons.overview: Students plan camera angles, text placement, and scene transitions for a simple animation or ad.
lessons.objective1: Use frames to communicate timing and flow.
lessons.objective2: Plan on-screen text for clarity and impact.
lessons.objective3: Refine storyboard with feedback.

lessons.title: Motion Graphics Basics
lessons.overview: Students learn animation fundamentals (keyframes, transitions, timing) and animate a logo or tagline.
lessons.objective1: Animate simple movements using keyframes.
lessons.objective2: Use easing and timing to improve feel.
lessons.objective3: Export short animations correctly.

lessons.title: Multimedia Ad Project (Quarter Capstone)
lessons.overview: Students combine branding, layout, and animation to produce a 15–30 second advertisement with sound, text, and motion.
lessons.objective1: Produce a short branded video with titles.
lessons.objective2: Integrate sound and motion coherently.
lessons.objective3: Revise based on peer and teacher feedback.

lessons.title: Gallery Showcase & Reflection
lessons.overview: Students present finished multimedia projects, reflect on the design process, and give/receive peer feedback.
lessons.objective1: Present work and articulate design choices.
lessons.objective2: Evaluate peers using a clear rubric.
lessons.objective3: Reflect on growth and next steps.
